content.problemStatement:
Chef has an integer sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. For each index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), Chef needs to divide AiA_iAi​ into two positive integers xxx and yyy (ie. x,y>0x, y > 0x,y>0) such that x+y=Aix + y = A_ix+y=Ai​, then place this as a point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) in the infinite 222-dimensional coordinate plane. Help Chef to find the maximum number of distinct points that can be put in the plane, if he optimally splits the values AiA_iAi​. Note that Chef can only perform one split for each index.

**Note:** Please use fast input/output methods for this problem.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum number of distinct points there can be in the infinite plane.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the length of the sequence. Second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible number of distinct points.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For sum 2 we have three occurrences but only 1 possible point, so contributes 1. For sum 4 we have two occurrences and 3 possible points, contributes 2. For sum 6 one occurrence contributes 1. Total 4. In the second test case each sum appears once, both contribute 1, total 2.

hidden.label: maximum boundary (few elements but large values)

solution.approach: For a fixed sum S, the possible points are (1,S‑1), (2,S‑2), …, (S‑1,1) – exactly S‑1 different points. If the value S appears cnt[S] times in the array, we can choose at most min(cnt[S], S‑1) distinct points for those occurrences. The answer is the sum of this value over all distinct sums. So we just count frequencies of each Ai and accumulate min(freq, Ai‑1). The algorithm is O(N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the sequence.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(K) where K is the number of distinct Ai (at most 100000).
